content.problemStatement:
Anas is playing an amazing game on a grid with NNN rows and MMM columns. The rows are numbered 111 through NNN from top to bottom and the columns are numbered 111 through MMM from left to right.

Anas wants to destroy this grid. To do that, he wants to send two heroes from the top left cell to the bottom right cell:


The first hero visits cells in row-major order: (1,1)‚Üí(1,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(1,M)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(2,M)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,M)(1,1) \rightarrow (1,2) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (1,M) \rightarrow (2,1) \rightarrow (2,2) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (2,M) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (N,M)(1,1)‚Üí(1,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(1,M)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(2,M)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,M).
The second hero visits cells in column-major order: (1,1)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,1)‚Üí(1,2)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,M)(1,1) \rightarrow (2,1) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (N,1) \rightarrow (1,2) \rightarrow (2,2) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (N,2) \rightarrow \ldots \rightarrow (N,M)(1,1)‚Üí(2,1)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,1)‚Üí(1,2)‚Üí(2,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,2)‚Üí‚Ä¶‚Üí(N,M).
We know that each hero destroys the first cell he visits, rests in the next KKK cells he visits without destroying them, then destroys the next cell he visits, rests in the next KKK cells, destroys the next cell, and so on until he reaches (and rests in or destroys) the last cell he visits.

Anas does not know the value of KKK. Therefore, for each value of KKK between 000 and N‚čÖM‚ąí1N \cdot M - 1N‚čÖM‚ąí1 inclusive, he wants to calculate the number of cells that will be destroyed by at least one hero. Can you help him?
constraints: the sum of N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚čÖ106
the sum of N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM space-separated integers as described above.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

K=0K = 0K=0: All cells will be destroyed by the heroes.
K=1K = 1K=1: The first hero will destroy the cells [(1,1),(1,3),(2,2)][(1,1), (1,3), (2,2)][(1,1),(1,3),(2,2)], while the second one will destroy the cells [(1,1),(1,2),(1,3)][(1,1), (1,2), (1,3)][(1,1),(1,2),(1,3)].
K=2K = 2K=2: The first hero will destroy the cells [(1,1),(2,1)][(1,1), (2,1)][(1,1),(2,1)], while the second one will destroy the cells [(1,1),(2,2)][(1,1), (2,2)][(1,1),(2,2)].
K=3K = 3K=3: The first hero will destroy the cells [(1,1),(2,2)][(1,1), (2,2)][(1,1),(2,2)], while the second one will destroy the cells [(1,1),(1,3)][(1,1), (1,3)][(1,1),(1,3)].
K=4K = 4K=4: The first hero will destroy the cells [(1,1),(2,3)][(1,1), (2,3)][(1,1),(2,3)] and the second one will also destroy the cells [(1,1),(2,3)][(1,1), (2,3)][(1,1),(2,3)].
K=5K = 5K=5 : The first hero will destroy the cell (1,1)(1,1)(1,1) and the second one will also destroy the cell (1,1)(1,1)(1,1).
